steps.title: Identify the passage's reframing signal
steps.content: The passage rejects the gender-constraint reading as incomplete—it “leaves the presence of Valdez's male collaborator Sandoval unexplained”—and then signals “We should instead consider” the art establishment's institutional restriction on what types of Chicano artists it recognizes.

steps.title: Match the institutional pivot to the answer
steps.content: Choice D identifies the work as about “constraining aesthetic expectations placed on Chicano artists in general rather than on the social constraints placed on women specifically.” This is precisely the institutional reframing the passage signals. The other choices either retain the gender focus (A, B) or make muralists the problem (C). That matches choice D — D is correct.
